title: cleome leschenaultii
intro: Cleome leschenaultii, also known as the pink velvet bush or pink cleome, is a perennial flowering plant native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its vibrant pink or white flowers and soft, fuzzy foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin disorders, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Cleome leschenaultii include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, while herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments can be used to harness its medicinal properties. Additionally, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used in traditional cooking and as a natural dye, highlighting its versatility and potential uses in modern herbalism.
